sections.paragraph:
& שְׂמאֹול quadrilit.-

(1) the left side. (Arab. شَمْأَلُ, شَمَالُ the north, compare No. 3. Jo. Simonis supposes the left hand and side to have been so called from being covered; because of its being wrapped up in the outer garment thrown over the left shoulder; and that the right hand on the contrary was called יָמִין from יָמַן i.q. אָמַן because it was given as the pledge of faithfulness in promises; if this etymology were adopted, the signif. No. 2, would be primary.) מִשְׂמאֹל on the left, 1Ki 7:49 followed by a gen. or dat. to any one’s left, Gen 48:13 2Sa 16:6 1Ki 22:19 2Ch 4:8. שְׂמאֹל (accus.) Gen 13:9 Num 20:17, and עַל שְׂמאֹל Gen 24:49 2Sa 2:19, to the left.-יַד־שְׂמאֹל the left hand, pr. the hand of the left side, Jud. 3:21 Eze 39:3.

(2) without יָד, the left hand, Gen 48:14 Son 2:6 8:3 .

(3) the north, the north quarter, Job 23:9. Gen 14:15, מִשְׂמאֹל לְדַמֶּשֶׂק “to the north of Damascus.” As to the Arab. شَمْأَلُ see above.
